title: cleistocalyx operculatus
intro: Cleistocalyx operculatus, also known as the Koa-Haole plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cleistocalyx operculatus include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy in the region. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or it can be made into a decoction by boiling the roots and stems to release their medicinal properties.
